author: Sangharakshita
title: Contents of Pali Canon - Samannaphala Sutta
content:
SAMANNA-PHALA SUTTA
CONTENTS


DighaNikaya
Bimbisara and Ajatasattu
Samana (Sramana)
Ti Ratna Vandana; The six teachers
Raja Suspiciously approaches Mango Grove
Indian Etiquette
What is the advantage of becoming a recluse?
Criteria for success - religious, mundane
People need religious figures outside the system - Staretz and Sadhu
"A married man will do anything for money'"
Take what you need, give what you can.
Ethics and Manners
Purana Kassapa: "There is no after-effect or resultant of action or Karma"
Makkhali Gosala: "No root or reason for the defilement or purity of beings"
Cosmic Determinism
Kala-Yuga
Ajita-Kesa-Kambala: "materialism"
Sophists; Socrates and Plato
Druids - Stonehenge
Pakuda-Kaccayana: Existence broken down into Seven Elements
Nigantha-Mahavira: Doctrine of the Four-fold restraint
Jains
Sanjaya Belathi: No underlying principle in universe
Madyamikas and Nagarjuna
"The Fruits of a Recluse"
"We need more Milarepas"
"He preaches the truth, lovely in its origin,... development...consummation"
Tathagata - defined
"Loveliness" of Dharma
Communication of Dharma in 3 Stages
"A householder hears the teaching"
One's ordinary life is cramped and narrow
Right Livelihood
Going Forth in the Modern World
Faith as the basis of "Going Forth"
"So he lives the homeless life..."
"Keeping the rules of the Order"
"He is complete in observances regarding food and behaviour"
The principle of non-violence
Power Mode in less obvious forms
"Fierce Friendship" vs. Power Mode
Power Mode negates the spiritual community
"No private life in the Spiritual Community"
Use of force outside spiritual community
"Not taking the Not Given"
Sharing
Forms of "Adinnadana"
Abstaining from sexual intercourse
"Right Speech"
Keeping promises.
Telling lies
Reliability
Slander and Back-biting
Harsh Speech and Trivial Speech
Triviality as reassurance
"Injury to plants" - ecology
Moderation in eating
Mindful eating
Selfadornment
Dressing-up for Pujas and classes
"Abandoning" scruffiness
The observance of morality as overcoming unskilful states
The fruit of a recluse as "unmixed ease"
Danger of dependency on a positive environment.
The Sangha as a "cold shower-not a warm bath".
The Bhikkhu is "guarded in the doors of his senses.
Being aware of external impingement on the senses.
"Introduction of Mindfulness into one's whole life".
"Cultural Consumerism"
Impingements on the Mind.
Spontaneity is not reactivity or moodiness.
Spontaneity as inspiration
113Doing without newspapers in Tuscany.
Doing without produced by media.
"How is the bhikkhu mindful and aware?"
Divided awareness
Continuity of awareness
Awareness of purpose
Awareness of purpose of actions
Ordinary self-consciousness
Alienated Self-consciousness
Self-consciousness as awareness
"Hiri" (Shame) as positive self-consciousness.
Transformation of activities through Mindfulness.
Maintaining awareness in dream state.
Awareness olonging awareness into dreams.
Awareness of patterns in one's life.
"How is the Bhikkhu contented?"
Needs required for spiritual development.
'Needs' or 'Greeds'?
"Genuine Contentment"
Contentment at Tuscany Retreat.
"The loss of the present is the essence of alienation".
Kindness
Preparation for Meditation
"Suppression of the 5 Hindrances"
Specific measures for overcoming Hindrances.
Meditative State as a Natural State.
Egyptian vs. Greek view of exercise.
Spiritual life a natural expression.
Like a man discharging a debt.
Like a man freed from prison.
Like a slave freed from servitude.
Like a wealthy man reaching safety.
Destruction of Hindrances: Freedom.
"Giving up worldly miseries-taking up spiritual happiness".
"To the one who sees within himself the 5 Hindrances destroyed is born gladness."
The Positive Nidanas.
The First Dhyana.
The Firstof the spiritual life as a visible self-transformation.
The Second Dhyana - absence of mental activity (Third Dhyana - footnote).
"If your life was properly organised, you'd end up in the lst/2nd Dhyana".
You need both pleasure and mindfulness - Equanimity
The Fourth Dhyana.
Equanimity as happiness become stable.
"The point at which Insight arises".
Insight begins with seeing the body as Impermanent.
You can only control your Mind.
The dissociation of body and mind in aging and rebirth.
"The MindMade Body".
The 'Mindmade' body has supernormal powers.
The 'mindmade body' in the bardo.
Description of 'psychicpowers'.
Bhikkhu directs his mind to achieving psychic powers.
'Celestial hearing'.
Subtle senses and telepathy.
"Having understood his own mind, he comes to know the minds of other beings."
Picking up on others' feelings.
"Recollection of former lives".
Temperamental differences in developing psychic powers.
Seeing the workings of the Law of Karma.
The Four Noble Truths.
"Destructiion of the Asavas".
Ajatasattu Goes for Refuge.
The right conditions.
Effective Going for Refuge.
Purposes of Confession.
